methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuantumDiamonds is a seed-stage quantum computing company founded by Kevin Berghoff and Fleming Bruckmaier. The target sits within a sector represented by 3 public peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion. The company has raised €7M in seed funding plus secured a €152M facility commitment.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The quantum computing sector contains only 5 same-sector peers, but filtering for same-stage or adjacent-stage companies yielded fewer than the three datapoints required for reliable median analysis. An all-sector median approach would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of quantum computing business models, architectures, and development timelines across the available comparables.

Without a sufficient narrow-stage peer set and without additional valuation inputs (revenue multiples, DCF anchors, or transaction precedents), this analysis cannot defensibly triangulate to a single range. The valuation framework remains incomplete. To proceed, the analysis would require either: (1) expansion to adjacent stages within quantum computing if stage-specific data becomes available, (2) introduction of transaction or financing round data from the peer set, or (3) alternative methods anchored to development milestones or capital intensity metrics specific to quantum hardware. Current data constraints preclude quantitative synthesis.
